page: Amazon Web Services g5.12xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores based on the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, alongside 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. For acceleration, it includes four NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPUs providing a combined 89 GB of VRAM, supported by a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile. While single-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 10% according to stress-ng testing, multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth fall within the middle 50% average. Conversely, the server demonstrates exceptional capability in GPU-accelerated tasks, achieving top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models. Passmark memory and CPU encryption tests also rank in the top 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g5.12xlarge offers a dense consolidation of multiple GPUs and local NVMe storage on a single instance, reducing the need for multi-node clustering for moderately sized models. However, the lower single-core CPU performance represents a tradeoff for workloads reliant on single-threaded execution. This hardware configuration is optimized for large language model inference, graphics-intensive rendering, multi-threaded decompression, and database operations requiring local high-speed NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services g5.12xlarge is a graphics-intensive server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an AMD EPYC 7R32 x86_64 processor with 48 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. It integrates four NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPUs with 89 GB of total VRAM and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, backed by 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show a clear tradeoff: single-core CPU performance ranks in the bottom 10%, whereas GPU-driven LLM inference for prompt processing and text generation consistently reaches top-tier levels. Passmark memory and CPU encryption performance also rank in the top 25%. This configuration provides high resource density for workloads like large language model inference, multi-threaded data processing, and graphics rendering, offering a consolidated alternative to multi-node clusters.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, four NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPUs with 89 GB VRAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage for high-density parallel workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5.12xlarge features 48 vCPUs, four NVIDIA A10G GPUs, and 3800 GB NVMe storage, delivering top-tier LLM inference performance.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated platform combining four NVIDIA Ampere GPUs and local NVMe storage for demanding parallel computing and machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPUs with 89 GB total VRAM for parallel acceleration.
48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor and 192 GB DDR4 RAM.
3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Top-tier performance in large language model inference and prompt processing.
Tradeoff of lower single-core CPU performance balanced by strong multi-core capabilities.
Suited for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and database operations.
